title: Minimum Sufficient Mechanism Bypass
claim: A mandatory MCP/FSM/verifier-broker apparatus was required to preserve the workflow's safety, authority, evidence, and independence invariants.

observed_evidence.detail: The pre-cleanup instruction surfaces made a larger apparatus prerequisite for a bounded objective (preserve the discipline invariants): e.g. 'Call start_task before any non-trivial work', 'Run advisory verification via run_advisory_verifier only; never call the verifier directly', and direct routes labelled 'deprecated bypass'.

Cleanup Episode A (cap-processor 5a5b750, v5.com.ua 3069ad2, Concept-Arena ddad2d0) replaced that apparatus with a smaller tool-agnostic rule that retains every invariant it protected: no self-review, verifier independence, scoped evidence, explicit human authorization, transport honesty.

The invariant-preserving postimage is the witness that the larger apparatus had no unique necessity witness: the same invariants hold under the smaller rule, so no reproduced failure, missing invariant, compatibility constraint, or measured scale requirement justified making the larger mechanism prerequisite.

missing_evidence.detail: No reproduced failure mode, missing verifier independence, missing rollback/atomicity, missing evidence binding, mandatory compatibility constraint, measured operational-scale requirement, or confirmed multi-use demand is recorded that the smaller sufficient path did not already satisfy.
No evidence establishes that admitting the larger mechanism as prerequisite was required; the sufficient path was displaced (direct routes declared deprecated bypass) without such a basis.

required_action: Select the minimum sufficient path — the smallest currently evidenced admissible mechanism that satisfies the frozen objective and every active invariant (the tool-agnostic rule) — and admit the larger apparatus only once a unique necessity witness for it is produced (a reproduced failure, a missing invariant, a measured scale requirement, or a confirmed multi-use demand). Do not delete governance, verification, rollback, or evidence binding to go smaller.
notes: Historical classification: VERIFIED_INTERNAL_HISTORICAL_SCOPE (narrow) for the displacement predicate only. Primary evidence is Cleanup Episode A (the 'chore(ai): remove stale cap-probe governance' commits cap-processor 5a5b750, v5.com.ua 3069ad2, Concept-Arena ddad2d0), whose exact preimage/postimage pair shows a mandatory larger apparatus replaced by a smaller invariant-preserving rule. NOT claimed as fact: that any particular component of the apparatus caused a specific outage; that the apparatus never had value in any other context; any per-episode runtime timing, request id, or model call. Negative controls that must NOT be read as this incident: an added component with a verified unique necessity witness; architecture merely proposed and refused; complexity alone; a deterministic mechanism later adopted to replace a model-mediated one (the correct direction); a reserved-but-unimplemented operation cited as the sufficient path. This is not an authority-blind gateway decision (AICE-602), not an unenforced control (AICE-610), and not a self-hosting mutation deadlock (AICE-613): it is a larger mechanism made prerequisite without a unique necessity witness, displacing an established sufficient path.
